tools.name: execute-sops-from-ops
tools.description: Execute a standard operation procedure (SOPS). Pipeline variables are top-level arguments (names from list-sops-parameters). Optional "parameters" merges; top-level keys win.

_pipeline_variables.description: Extra top-level keys are pipeline variables; names match list-sops-parameters.

sops_id.description: ID of the SOPS procedure to execute

tools.name: list-sops-parameters-from-ops
tools.description: List all required parameters for a specific SOPS procedure

sops_id.description: ID of the SOPS procedure to get parameters for

tools.name: list-events-from-ops
tools.description: List available event types by querying the backend API. Supports search filtering and pagination to discover different event categories and patterns.

page_size.description: Number of event types to return (default: 10)

search.description: Search term to filter event types (optional)

tools.name: get-events-from-ops
tools.description: Get events using raw NATS subject patterns. Supports three query types: 1) Direct query (exact subject), 2) Wildcard query (using * for single level), 3) Prefix matching (using > for multi-level suffix). Examples: 'ops.clusters.{cluster}.namespaces.{namespace}.pods.{pod-name}.event', 'ops.clusters.*.namespaces.ops-system.webhooks.*', 'ops.clusters.*.namespaces.{namespace}.hosts.>'

start_time.description: Start time for filtering events (Unix epoch milliseconds)

subject_pattern.description: NATS subject pattern for event querying (supports wildcards * and > for flexible matching)

tools.name: list-metrics-from-prometheus
tools.description: List all available metrics from Prometheus. Returns metric names, types, and basic information.

limit.description: Maximum number of metrics to return (default: 100)

tools.name: query-metrics-from-prometheus
tools.description: Execute a custom PromQL instant query. Examples: 'up', 'cpu_usage_percent', 'sum(rate(ops_mcp_server_http_requests_total[5m]))'

tools.name: query-metrics-range-from-prometheus
tools.description: Execute a custom PromQL range query over a time period. Examples: 'rate(cpu_usage[5m])', 'sum(memory_usage_bytes) by (pod)'

step.description: Query resolution step (default: 15s, examples: 15s, 30s, 60s, 1m, 5m). Supports s(seconds), m(minutes), h(hours)

time_range.description: Time range for query (examples: 5m, 10m, 1h, 2h, 24h, 7d). Supports s(seconds), m(minutes), h(hours), d(days)

body.description: Complete Elasticsearch query body as JSON string. Supports all ES Query DSL features: query, aggs, size, from, sort, _source, etc. Example: '{"size":0,"query":{"query_string":{"query":"error"}},"aggs":{"by_level":{"terms":{"field":"level.keyword"}}}}'

index.description: Index name or pattern to search (e.g., 'logs-*', 'filebeat-*')

tools.name: list-log-indices-from-elasticsearch
tools.description: List all available log indices in Elasticsearch

health.description: Filter by health status (green, yellow, red)

tools.name: query-logs-from-elasticsearch
tools.description: Query logs using ES|QL (Elasticsearch Query Language)

columnar.description: Return results in columnar format (true or false) - default: false

format.description: Response format (json, csv, tsv, txt) - default: json

query.description: ES|QL query string. Example: 'FROM logs-* | WHERE @timestamp > NOW() - 1 hour | STATS count() BY level'

tools.name: get-services-from-jaeger
tools.description: Gets the service names as JSON array of string. No input parameter supported.

tools.name: get-operations-from-jaeger
tools.description: Gets the operations as JSON array of object with name and spanKind properties.

spanKind.description: Filters operations by OpenTelemetry span kind (server, client, producer, consumer, internal)

tools.name: get-trace-from-jaeger
tools.description: Gets the spans by the given trace by ID. Returns both original Jaeger format and converted OpenTelemetry format with standardized trace/span IDs and attributes.

endTime.description: The end time to filter spans in the RFC 3339, section 5.6 format, (e.g., 2017-07-21T17:32:28Z)

startTime.description: The start time to filter spans in the RFC 3339, section 5.6 format, (e.g., 2017-07-21T17:32:28Z)

traceId.description: Filters spans by OpenTelemetry compatible trace id in 32-character hexadecimal string format

tools.name: find-traces-from-jaeger
tools.description: Searches for traces based on criteria. Returns both original Jaeger format and converted OpenTelemetry format with standardized trace/span IDs and attributes.

durationMax.description: Maximum duration of a span in milliseconds

durationMin.description: Minimum duration of a span in milliseconds

operationName.description: The operation name to filter spans

startTimeMax.description: End of the time interval (exclusive) in the RFC 3339, section 5.6 format

startTimeMin.description: Start of the time interval (inclusive) in the RFC 3339, section 5.6 format
